name: Republic of the Congo (Congo-Brazzaville / République du Congo)
summary: The Republic of the Congo (Congo-Brazzaville) is a Central African civil-law unitary republic whose family-law framework operates under the Family Code 1984 (Code de la Famille, Law 73/84 of 17 October 1984) drawing on French civil-law substantive heritage with codification of customary-law marriage provisions. Parental authority and child custody are governed by Family Code arts. 322-345. The Supreme Court (Cour Suprême) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters; the Constitutional Court (Cour Constitutionnelle) operates separate constitutional review. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Court of First Instance (Tribunal de Grande Instance). Psychology profession is regulated through the Ministry of Health framework. Republic of the Congo is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the interest-of-the-child standard. Republic of the Congo acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 March 2009.

statutory_framework.title: Family Code — Parental authority and custody
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Family Code drawing on French civil-law substantive heritage with codification of customary-law marriage provisions. Arts. 322-345 govern parental authority and child custody.

statutory_framework.title: Law on Child Protection
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Law on Child Protection aligned with UNCRC obligations.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court (Cour Suprême)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Constitutional Court (Cour Constitutionnelle)
apex_courts.role: Constitutional Court with original jurisdiction over constitutional review.

professional_regulators.name: Ministry of Health, Republic of the Congo
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Congolese family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Congo-Brazzaville independence + Fulbert Youlou founding-presidency
key_developments.description: Republic of the Congo independence 15 August 1960 from France — Fulbert Youlou first president 1960-1963 substantively distinctive Central African founding-presidency framework. Foundational pre-Marxist-Leninist framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: People's Republic of the Congo + Marien Ngouabi + Marxist-Leninist framework
key_developments.description: People's Republic of the Congo (République populaire du Congo) established 31 December 1969 under Marien Ngouabi — substantively distinctive Central African Marxist-Leninist framework 1969-1992. Substantive Cold-War-era political-institutional framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and family-law-modernisation.

key_developments.title: Sassou-Nguesso first presidency + extended-presidential framework
key_developments.description: Denis Sassou-Nguesso assumed presidency 8 February 1979 — substantively distinctive Central African extended-presidency framework 1979-1992 (13 years) + 1997-present (29 years and continuing through 2026) = 42 years total. Substantively distinctive globally one of longest-current-presidencies-in-Africa framework.

key_developments.title: Family Code 1984 (Law 73/84)
key_developments.description: Federal Family Code enacted 17 October 1984 drawing on French civil-law substantive heritage with codification of customary-law marriage provisions. Arts. 322-345 govern parental authority and child custody. Foundational substantive-family-law framework persisting through subsequent regime-changes.

key_developments.title: Republic of the Congo ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: Republic of the Congo ratified the UNCRC on 14 October 1993 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration with subsequent 2010 Law on Child Protection substantive doctrine.

key_developments.title: Sovereign National Conference + democratic-transition + Lissouba framework
key_developments.description: Congo Sovereign National Conference 25 February – 10 June 1991 substantively distinctive Central African democratic-transition framework. Pascal Lissouba elected President August 1992 ending Sassou-Nguesso first presidency. Subsequent Constitution of Congo-Brazzaville 1992 establishing democratic-trajectory framework.

key_developments.title: Congo Civil War 1997-1999 + Sassou-Nguesso return + armed-conflict framework
key_developments.description: Congo Civil War 5 June 1997 – December 1999 — Sassou-Nguesso militia (Cobras) defeated Lissouba forces. Sassou-Nguesso resumed presidency 25 October 1997 — substantively distinctive Central African return-to-power-via-civil-war framework. Substantive armed-conflict framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Constitution of Congo 2002 + post-civil-war presidential-republic framework
key_developments.description: Constitution of Congo 2002 adopted 20 January 2002 substantively reforming political-institutional framework establishing post-civil-war presidential-republic framework. Substantive constitutional anchor for contemporary family-law jurisprudence.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 accession
key_developments.description: Republic of the Congo acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 March 2009 — substantively significant Hague Central African accession (earlier than most regional peers) affecting cross-border-jurisdiction-practice with France and other Hague-party states.

key_developments.title: CEMAC framework + Conseil Consultatif des Droits de l'Homme + Sassou-Nguesso institutional consolidation
key_developments.description: Republic of the Congo continuing CEMAC (Communauté Économique et Monétaire de l'Afrique Centrale) framework substantively significant cross-border family-law adjudication. Conseil Consultatif des Droits de l'Homme institutional position addressing PA-related issues within Family Code 1984 + Law on Child Protection 2010 framework. Denis Sassou-Nguesso 42-year continuing-presidency consolidation 2023. Substantively significant Central African institutional context.

key_developments.title: Cour Suprême + Cour Constitutionnelle — interest-of-the-child substantive register + Sassou-Nguesso 42-year continuing-presidency
key_developments.description: Cour Suprême and Cour Constitutionnelle continue to develop interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Family Code 1984 arts. 322-345 + Law on Child Protection 2010 + Hague Convention 1980 framework in custody disputes within Sassou-Nguesso 42-year continuing-presidency framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Republic of the Congo operates a French-civil-law family-law framework with codification of customary-law marriage provisions — places Congo-Brazzaville in the Central African Francophone civil-law cluster with structurally distinctive French-derivative substantive heritage.
Hague Convention 1980 accession 2009 places Republic of the Congo in the Hague Central African cluster — earlier than most peers.

sources.title: Supreme Court of the Republic of the Congo

editorial_notes: Republic of the Congo jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 3 to 10 key_developments with full Youlou-to-Sassou-Nguesso trajectory: 1960-Congo-Brazzaville-independence-+-Youlou + 1969-People's-Republic-+-Ngouabi-+-Marxist-Leninist + 1979-Sassou-Nguesso-first-presidency + 1984-Family-Code-Law-73-84 + 1990-1993-UNCRC-ratification + 1991-Sovereign-National-Conference-+-Lissouba-democratic-transition + 1997-Congo-Civil-War-+-Sassou-Nguesso-return + 2002-Constitution-of-Congo-+-post-civil-war + 2009-Hague-Convention-1980-accession + 2024-Cour-Suprême-+-Sassou-Nguesso-42-year-continuing-presidency.
Civil-law Central African Francophone (French-derivative + Family Code 1984 + Law on Child Protection 2010 + Hague Convention 1980 accession 2009).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive interest-of-the-child analysis under Family Code 1984 arts. 322-345 + Law on Child Protection 2010 + Hague Convention 1980 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Central-African + Francophone + civil-law + French-derivative + Sassou-Nguesso-42-year-continuing-presidency-1979-1992-+-1997-2026-one-of-longest-current-presidencies-in-Africa-distinctive + People's-Republic-Marxist-Leninist-1969-1992 + Congo-Sovereign-National-Conference-1991 + Congo-Civil-War-1997-1999-Cobras-return-to-power-via-civil-war + Constitution-of-Congo-2002 + Hague-Convention-1980-accession-2009-earlier-than-most-Central-African-peers clusters within the corpus.
